ANY PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS MUST BE IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG, SPECIFYING THE DATE(S) OF COMMUNICATION OR TRANSMISSION OF THE DOCUMENT, THE AUTHOR AND RECIPIENTS OF THE DOCUMENT, THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE DOCUMENT, AND THE BASIS FOR CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Courtney Brown, Esq., 415-371-9114, appearing for Brett Penfil, Plaintiff and moving party.

Joseph Tursi, Esq. of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, 415-278-9400, appearing for Susan Badger, for Defendant and opposing party.

Hearing is held. Ms. Brown stated that there may still be outstanding invoices, that plaintiff's requested amended responses and that parties are on the same page in terms of documents. The Court reported that this case involves a unit damaged by water. Mr. Tursi stated that he is happy to amend the verified responses.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Susan Badger'S Notice Motion And Motion To Modify Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold (415-533-0735) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Brett Penfil.
Jeanne Marie Lechich (415-278-9400) for Defendant/Moving party SUSAN BADGER.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Both sides submit to the tentative ruling. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUSAN BADGER'S Motion To Modify Subpoena For Production Of Business Records is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is granted as follows: Third-party witness, Mark Bran Architectural, may redact any financial information or social security number form its production. Any privileged documents must be identified in a privilege log, specifying the date(s) of communication or transmission of the document, the author and recipients of the document, the subject matter of the document, and the basis for claim of privilege. Motion is otherwise denied. Documents to be produced within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
